organization_name: Insane Asylum of the State of California
line: Insane Asylum of the State of California. Located between California and Sacramento streets, and extending northerly from Park street thirty-six blocks, the grounds containing 107 acres, nearly all of which was donated to the State by Capt. C. M. Weber in 1851, the date of the foundation of the present institution. The buildings are large and commodious, with facilities for the accommodation of about 1150 patients. H. N. Rucker, M. D., medical superintendent; W. A. Washington, M. D. and J. D. Young, assistant physicians; H. W. Taylor, superintendent's secretary; Mrs. E. Ramsell, matron; Walter M. Trivett, apothecary; T. J. Keyes, steward; J. Watson, supervisor.
Pacific Hospital. Asa Clark, M. D., proprietor. South street, corner Center.

County Hospital. Located one mile from the Court House, on East street. Dr. S. P. Crawford, physician; Charles W. Ward, steward; Mrs. Etta L. Ward, matron. Under the control and management of the Board of Supervisors of the county. The expense of the institution is about $20,000 per annum. Average number of inmates about one hundred and twenty-five.
